title: Terraform - an automation Tool
author: Priya
content:
Take Quiz

What is Terraform?

Terraform is a IAC tool which is a provider of a wide number of cloud ,database and provisioning of other core components to develop a production ready workspace by off-loading all your work of server and backend management at the providers end.It allows easy connectivity and handling with seamless integration of different modules and componets with our project.Low cost and simplification of connecting with different analytics and web tools r some of biggest insights of using Terraform as a service provisioning tool
Terraform Components -
its 2 important components -
.

  1. Core - This component takes data from 2 input sources to plan for a best provisioning of tools and other components that r required.

    1. ## config file -that contains data of how to configure/manage the service .It also contains information about the desired state of the system that how our infrastructure should look like. What needs to be created, deleted or any updation in the existing data r things under config file. It has .tf extension and uses hashcorp config language. Can also write our configuration setup in json format but HCL format is more readable.
    2. ## State - the current state of setup that tells how our infrastructure looks like.
    Terraform core compares the current state data to that of the desired state of config file to make that setup for infrastructure provisioning.
  2. providers - a provider is a plugin that interacts with cloud providers, third-party tools, and other APIs. Terraform Registry hosts over 3,000 providers .

In Terraform IAC means we can create our infrastructure with a simple code and config file. IAC means use of simple code to create, manage computer infrastructure like - servers , network, storage components without any manual setup just by writing some simple script in HCL format.
Terraform commands in config file -
initialise ,plan ,apply/destroy r 3 step commands for connecting to any cloud base resource or other services like of docker , kubernetes.

  • terraform init
  • terraform plan { we can see all the changes that would appear after using that tool/resource)
  • terraform apply/destroy

If u want live server support for your website without going through any expensive cost plan of taking hosting of other domain provider like - hostinger, GoDaddy than u can simply import your code in Terraform cloud and use above 3 config commands and u will get a link to make your site live in a second without any pricing plan or monthly subscription.


Terraform Vs Ansible Vs Jenkins

Terraform = Server Provisioning tool . Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tool for provisioning and managing infrastructure (e.g., virtual machines, networks, databases, load balancers)
Ansible = Configuration Management for servers that you create through Server Provisioning and do all Continuous deployment into the production server.
Jenkins during its code pipelining building process uses tools like Terraform and Ansible to automate infrastructure provisioning, configuration, and application deployment.

Both Terraform and ansible r infrastructure as a code(IAC) tool but ansible only helps in managing and configuring the platform/infrastructure that we r using to make our service run or deploy but do not provide any tools or platform where we deploy our application.Any server need , VPC , or any containerisation tool for our app r provided by terraform .
